summary.title: Car Buying Patterns
summary.description: Car buying patterns have changed over the years, with the mean selling price increasing from 0.6 to 11.26 between 1992 and 2021. The average age of cars being purchased has decreased from 31 to 2 years over the same period. The mean km driven has also decreased from 22612 to 8800.

insights.title: Increasing Mean Selling Price
insights.description: The mean selling price has increased from 0.6 to 11.26 between 1992 and 2021, indicating a growth opportunity in the car market. This increase is likely driven by the increasing demand for newer cars, with the average age of cars being purchased decreasing from 31 to 2 years over the same period.

insights.title: Contradiction in Fuel Type Distribution
insights.description: The fuel type distribution shows a significant increase in petrol cars, but the mean selling price for diesel cars (8.23) is higher than for petrol cars (5.03). This contradiction may indicate a shift in consumer preferences or a change in market trends.

insights.title: Decreasing Mean Km Driven
insights.description: The mean km driven has decreased from 22612 to 8800 between 1992 and 2021, indicating a pattern of decreasing car usage over time. This decrease may be driven by the increasing popularity of newer cars, which are likely to have lower mileage.

insights.title: Skewed Age Distribution
insights.description: The age distribution is skewed, with a skewness of 0.87, indicating that the data is not normally distributed. This skewness may be driven by the decreasing average age of cars being purchased over time, with the average age decreasing from 31 to 2 years between 1992 and 2021.

hypotheses.justification: The correlation between year and selling price is 0.39, indicating a positive relationship. The mean selling price has increased from 0.6 to 11.26 between 1992 and 2021.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cars with 'Diesel' fuel type have significantly higher 'selling_price' than cars with 'Petrol' fuel type
hypotheses.justification: The mean selling price for diesel cars (8.23) is higher than for petrol cars (5.03), indicating a significant difference in selling price between the two fuel types.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The 'age' column follows a skewed distribution
hypotheses.justification: The skewness of the age distribution is 0.87, indicating that the data is not normally distributed. This skewness may be driven by the decreasing average age of cars being purchased over time.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The 'km_driven' metric shows a seasonal pattern with higher values in certain 'year' values
hypotheses.justification: The mean km driven has decreased from 22612 to 8800 between 1992 and 2021, indicating a decreasing trend over time. There is no evidence of a seasonal pattern with higher values in certain year values.

hypotheses.hypothesis: The 'engine' column is positively correlated with the 'max_power' column
hypotheses.justification: The correlation between engine and max power is 0.79, indicating a strong positive relationship. This suggests that cars with more powerful engines tend to have higher max power values.

query: Is there any change in car buying pattern over the years
